name: Masoud Pezeshkian
summary: Day 52 Sunday. SNSC OPERATIONAL CONSOLIDATION IS INSTITUTIONAL RE-ALIGNMENT AGAINST PEZESHKIAN'S DIPLOMATIC TRACK. The Supreme National Security Council — which Pezeshkian himself chairs — issued its April 18 evening statement formally endorsing the IRGC Hormuz-closure posture that he had appointed Araghchi to oppose. Pezeshkian's own civilian apex has now formally endorsed the override of his Foreign Minister's Friday framework. Araghchi (Pezeshkian's appointee) is being openly rebuked by Kayhan / Fars / Tasnim with named 'complete poor judgment' language; open public rebuke of Araghchi is INDIRECT REBUKE of Pezeshkian's diplomatic track. Pezeshkian has NOT publicly defended Araghchi as of Sunday 09:00 IST — silence indicates DURABLE HARDLINER CONSOLIDATION, not factional re-opening. Pattern holds: civilian-wing state media (IRNA) quiet non-defence; Pezeshkian quiet non-defence. The three-way civilian/IRGC/Supreme Leader split is operationally collapsed for the first time since March 20. Falsifiable: if Pezeshkian publicly defends Araghchi within 48 hours, factional re-opening; if resignation / reassignment / public muzzle of Araghchi within 48 hours, hardliner ascendancy goes to personnel level.

signals.content: FIRST DISTINCT PEZESHKIAN SIGNAL. Pezeshkian set conditions for ceasefire: US/Israel must recognize Iran's rights, pay reparations, guarantee no future war. Positioning is distinct from FM Araghchi (categorical rejection) — framing Iran as having conditional willingness to end war. Early indication of civilian government's dealmaking orientation that later becomes the IRNA line diverging from the Tasnim/IRGC line. (Sources: Al Jazeera)

'NECESSARY WILL' SIGNAL TO EU [RHETORIC — most conciliatory Iran civilian language since Day 1]. President Pezeshkian told EU Council President António Costa that Iran has 'the necessary will' to end the war. This was the most conciliatory language from Iran's civilian government since Day 1 — deliberately timed ahead of Trump's primetime address to American people. (Sources: EU Council readout, Al Jazeera, Reuters)

OPEN LETTER TO AMERICAN PEOPLE [RHETORIC]. Pezeshkian published an open letter to the American people on X/Twitter on April 1: 'The world stands at a crossroads. Continuing along the path of confrontation is more costly and futile than ever.' Released hours before Trump's primetime address — deliberate counter-programming. CREDIBILITY CAVEAT: Per The Week India, Pezeshkian is in 'complete political deadlock' — IRGC blocks presidential appointments, controls information flow between Pezeshkian and Supreme Leader. 'IRGC effectively governing while civilian government sends peace signals with no executive authority to deliver.' The peace signals are genuine positioning but may be operationally unenforceable. This is the CENTRAL credibility problem of the civilian track: the person making conciliatory statements may not have authority to commit Iran. (Sources: X/Twitter, Al Jazeera, The Week India)

TRAUMA DISCLOSURE TO SHARIF [REPORTED]. Pezeshkian told Pakistani PM Sharif that he was attacked twice during previous nuclear talks — a personal trauma disclosure that explains civilian-side demand for confidence-building measures (strike pause) before direct talks. Creates a circular dependency with US position (US wants talks before pause; Iran civilian wants pause before talks). This disclosure is significant for two reasons: (1) it personalizes Pezeshkian's resistance to face-to-face diplomacy, (2) it provides a genuine justification for proximity format later adopted in Islamabad April 11. The IRGC would not view personal attacks on civilian negotiators as disqualifying in the same way. (Sources: Dawn, Al Jazeera, The Hill)

PEACE SIGNALS MARGINALIZED POST-F15-SHOOTDOWN. Iran media tone shifted to TRIUMPHALIST after F-15 shootdown — IRGC stream now dominant over Pezeshkian peace signals in state media coverage. Three-way internal split persists (civilian vs IRGC vs reformist media) but IRGC winning the narrative war. Pezeshkian peace signals still exist but are 'increasingly marginalized.' This is the first visible erosion of the civilian track's media presence and foreshadows the Day 39 deadline-day silence. (Sources: Iran International, Al Jazeera, Tasnim, IRNA)

DEADLINE-DAY SILENCE [CONFIRMED ABSENCE]. Pezeshkian was SILENT on April 7, Trump's final-deadline day — may indicate civilian government has been functionally sidelined by the IRGC-SNSC axis. The hardline delegation speakers (Ghalibaf, Azizi, Araghchi) dominated Iran's rhetorical response to Trump's ultimatum. FDD analysis identifies Ghalibaf as one of '5 men now running Iran.' The absence of Pezeshkian from the most consequential day of the crisis is structurally significant: he either chose silence (tactical), was blocked from speaking (power dynamic), or assessed his voice would be counterproductive to civilian goals. All three readings point to civilian marginalization. (Sources: Al Jazeera liveblog, FDD, Iran International)

PEZESHKIAN-TAEB CONFRONTATION [REPORTED — Iran International single-source cluster, later corroborated by Ynet]. President Pezeshkian confronted IRGC commander Hossein Taeb on April 4 in a 'tense and highly charged exchange,' accusing IRGC commanders Ahmad Vahidi and Ali Abdollahi of 'acting unilaterally and driving escalation through attacks on regional countries.' Pezeshkian warned Iran's economy 'would not be able to withstand a prolonged war.' IRGC reportedly rejected Pezeshkian's intelligence minister candidates and forced its own appointment; sources described Guards as having 'effectively stripped the government of executive control.' Treat as REPORTED until fully corroborated — but the pattern is consistent with prior The Week India 'complete political deadlock' reporting. This is the FIRST explicit civilian-military confrontation publicly documented in the crisis and provides structural reading for the IRNA-Tasnim media divergence. (Sources: Iran International, Ynet, The Week India)

Day 44. IRGC POWER GRAB DEEPENED — 'TOTAL COLLAPSE 3-4 WEEKS' WARNING [REPORTED — Iran International + NewsX single-source cluster, NOT independently confirmed]. Per Iran International and NewsX: IRGC chief Ahmad Vahidi has blocked ALL of Pezeshkian's nominees for intelligence minister, including Hossein Dehghan, stating 'all critical and sensitive leadership positions must be selected and managed directly by the IRGC until further notice.' Pezeshkian is in 'complete political deadlock' — accused Vahidi and Abdollahi of 'acting unilaterally and driving escalation through attacks on regional countries' and warned those policies 'had destroyed any remaining chance of a ceasefire' and were steering Iran toward 'a huge catastrophe.' CRITICAL FALSIFIABLE CLAIM: warned the economy could face 'total collapse within three weeks to one month' without a ceasefire — this gives an early-May testable deadline for civilian assessment. Pezeshkian has made 'repeated requests' for meetings with Mojtaba Khamenei that have 'all gone unanswered' — a security cordon surrounds Mojtaba, preventing government reports from reaching him. This raises THE critical question for Islamabad: does the Iranian delegation (Ghalibaf, Araghchi) actually have authority to commit Iran, or will any agreement face IRGC veto? IRNA has NOT reported these claims; Tasnim has NOT addressed them. Sourcing is opposition/diaspora (Iran International) + Indian outlet (NewsX). Treat institutional claims as REPORTED. (Sources: Iran International, NewsX, Atalayar, Soufan Center)

Pezeshkian told French President Macron Iran will negotiate 'within framework of international law' — keeping the European diplomatic channel alive (CONFIRMED statement — source: PressTV, Middle East Eye, Barron's/AFP). Domestic messaging: 'national resistance shattered enemy dream of collapse' (source: IRNA). PressTV English service led with Pezeshkian's Macron call and international law framing — aimed at European audiences, not domestic. The shift from unified 'US overreach' framing to differentiated multi-track response: IRGC threats (Tasnim), diplomatic signaling (IRNA), European engagement (PressTV/Pezeshkian). This differentiation may reflect absence of Mojtaba Khamenei's unified direction (wounded/incapacitated) or deliberate multi-track strategy.

SNSC OPERATIONAL ENDORSEMENT OF IRGC HORMUZ POSTURE IS INDIRECT REBUKE OF PEZESHKIAN'S OWN DIPLOMATIC TRACK [CONFIRMED — SNSC statement institutional; PEZESHKIAN SILENCE non-defence]. Pezeshkian chairs the Supreme National Security Council by statute. The SNSC's April 18 evening statement operationalizing IRGC Hormuz-closure enforcement and re-imposing $1/bbl transit fees was issued WHILE Pezeshkian chairs that body — his civilian apex has formally endorsed the IRGC position he had appointed Araghchi to oppose. Openly-rebuked FM Araghchi is Pezeshkian's own appointee; Kayhan/Fars/Tasnim's named rebuke of Araghchi as 'complete poor judgment in communication' (Iran International, Al Jazeera, NCRI) is indirect rebuke of Pezeshkian's diplomatic track. Pezeshkian has NOT publicly defended Araghchi as of Sunday 09:00 IST — silence is intelligence. IRNA (civilian-wing state wire) quiet non-defence; Pezeshkian quiet non-defence. Classic accommodation pattern sustained for second consecutive day. Falsifiable (Sadjadpour): if Pezeshkian defends Araghchi within 48 hours, factional re-opening; if Araghchi is resigned / reassigned / publicly muzzled within 48 hours, hardliner ascendancy advances to personnel level, not just operational. (Sources: Press TV, Tasnim, Al Jazeera, Iran International, NCRI)

edges.context: Day 52. SNSC (which Pezeshkian chairs) endorsed IRGC Hormuz posture April 18 — civilian apex formally endorsing override of his own FM's Friday framework. Pezeshkian has NOT publicly defended Araghchi Sunday 09:00 IST. Silence indicates durable hardliner consolidation, not factional re-opening. Three-way civilian/IRGC/Supreme Leader split operationally collapsed first time since March 20.

Day 52. Pezeshkian-chaired SNSC endorsed IRGC Hormuz-closure posture April 18 — hardliner consolidation institutionalised in body Pezeshkian nominally leads. Not active confrontation this weekend; accommodation / absorption. Open Kayhan/Fars/Tasnim rebuke of Araghchi (Pezeshkian's appointee) is indirect rebuke of Pezeshkian. Silence = accommodation, not resistance.

Pezeshkian's 'repeated requests' for meetings with Mojtaba have 'all gone unanswered.' Security cordon around Mojtaba preventing government access. Cannot function as civilian executive when blocked from the Supreme Leader. This edge captures the institutional isolation, not a relationship of cooperation.

Told Pakistani PM Sharif about personal trauma from prior nuclear talks (two attacks). Civilian government track for Islamabad mediation. Pezeshkian is the principal who would need to ratify any deal Ghalibaf brings back — but his authority is contested.

Told EU Council President Costa Iran has 'necessary will' to end war. Published open letter to American people on X. Never directly contacted US officials. Indirect diplomacy through European channel.

trigger_points.condition: Pezeshkian resigns, is removed, or publicly denounces the IRGC power grab
trigger_points.mechanism: Resignation or public break would signal the civilian track has collapsed entirely and IRGC rule is explicit. Would make any Islamabad framework un-ratifiable on the Iran side. Oil spikes, VIX surges, Gulf defense premium rises. Conversely, a public reconciliation photo-op with Mojtaba would signal internal reconciliation and strengthen delegation authority.

trigger_points.condition: Pezeshkian's 'total collapse within three weeks to one month' economic warning proves correct (early-May deadline)
trigger_points.mechanism: The specific falsifiable claim (April 11 + 3 weeks = May 2; + 1 month = May 11) creates a testable window. If Iranian economic indicators (rial collapse, bread/fuel rationing, unemployment spike) validate his warning, it confirms civilian-track credibility on domestic assessment and pressures the IRGC to accept any Islamabad framework. If the economy holds, the IRGC narrative (war is sustainable) wins the internal argument.

trigger_points.condition: Pezeshkian publicly meets or is photographed with Mojtaba Khamenei
trigger_points.mechanism: Would simultaneously resolve the Mojtaba proof-of-life question AND signal the civilian-IRGC split is being repaired. Strongest single de-escalation signal because it would confirm Ghalibaf's delegation has mandate from the Supreme Leader channel, not just the IRGC military council. Oil crashes 5-8% in a session.
